summary: reclaimIfExpired no longer releases FileGuard's hold; it only force-reverts the catalog Status (Splitting->Active) to unblock AdmitWrite. reclaimIfExpired and EndSplit now both hold Orchestrator.mu across their own transitionStatus call, so the two can never interleave catalog read-then-writes for the same fileID. leases map values changed from bare time.Time deadlines to {deadline, gen, reclaimed} entries. BeginSplit no longer retries TryAcquire after a reclaim. WithClock/WithLeaseDuration unexported to withClock/withLeaseDuration (non-blocking note).

residual_gap_for_verifier_attention.description: A fileID whose true split holder has genuinely crashed (never calls EndSplit/AbortSplit) now has its FileGuard hold permanently stuck: future BeginSplit calls keep returning ErrAlreadySplitting indefinitely. Writers remain unblocked (AdmitWrite succeeds) since AdmitWrite only consults catalog Status, which reclaimIfExpired still correctly reverts.
residual_gap_for_verifier_attention.why_left_open: Full closure without reopening the double-acquisition hole requires either (a) an ownership/fencing token added to FileGuard.TryAcquire/Release (engine/split/guard.go -- explicitly out of this fix-cycle's file scope, per the task's own 'stop and report' instruction), or (b) threading such a token through BeginSplit/EndSplit's own signatures (blocked by engine/split/split_race_test.go's existing calls with today's fileID-only signatures, which this fix-cycle is not permitted to touch).
residual_gap_for_verifier_attention.recommendation: A follow-up subtask with file scope including engine/split/guard.go, engine/split/guard_test.go, engine/split/orchestrate.go, engine/split/orchestrate_test.go, and engine/split/split_race_test.go should add a caller-opaque fencing token: FileGuard.TryAcquire returns one, FileGuard.Release requires the matching one, and BeginSplit/EndSplit thread it through their own signatures, updating split_race_test.go's call sites in the same change.

escape_hatch_note: guard.go was read but not modified; a guard.go change was determined necessary for FULL closure of the residual gap above, but the achievable-without-guard.go fix (eliminating the double-acquisition itself) was implemented per the task's request, and the guard.go-requiring residual is disclosed rather than silently left unaddressed or worked around by touching guard.go.
